title: Martand Sun Temple
tagline: Martand Sun Temple — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: The Martand Sun Temple is a Hindu temple located in Mattan, in the Anantnag district of the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir, India.
overview.description: The Martand Sun Temple is a Hindu temple located in Mattan, in the Anantnag district of the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It dates back to the eighth century CE and was dedicated to Surya, the solar deity in Hinduism; Surya is also known by the Sanskrit-language synonym Martand (मार्तण्ड, Mārtaṇḍa). The temple was destroyed by Sikandar Shah Miri.

topPlaces.name: Krewa Rampur
topPlaces.description: Krewa Rampur is a village in Anantnag tehsil in Anantnag district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. Krewa Rampur village is located in Anantnag Tehsil of Anantnag district in Jammu & Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Chittisinghpura massacre
topPlaces.description: The Chittisinghpura massacre refers to the mass murder of 35 Sikh villagers on 20 March 2000 in the village of Chittisinghpura in Anantnag district, Jammu and Kashmir, India on the eve of the U.S. President Bill Clinton's state visit to India. The Indian government asserts that the massacre was conducted by the Pakistan-based militant group Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) or Hizbul Mujahideen.

topPlaces.name: Bumzuva Cave and Temple
topPlaces.description: Bumzuva cave and temple are a group of Hindu temples and man-made caves in the village of Bumazuv in the Lidder Valley of Anantnag district in Jammu and Kashmir, India.

topPlaces.name: Lidder Valley
topPlaces.description: The Lidder Valley or Liddar Valley is a Himalayan sub-valley of the Kashmir Valley, located in the Anantnag district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. The Lidder River flows down the valley.

topPlaces.name: Mattan, Jammu and Kashmir
topPlaces.description: Mattan (originally, Martand) is a town, tehsil and a notified area committee, near Anantnag city in the Anantnag district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Devi Pora
topPlaces.description: Devi Pora, commonly known as Devipora, is a village in the Anantnag tehsil of Anantnag district in the Kashmir Valley of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is situated 17 km away from the town of Achabal.

topPlaces.name: Hardu Toru
topPlaces.description: Hardu Toru, commonly known as Toor, is a village in Anantnag tehsils in Anantnag district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. Hardu Toru village is located in Anantnag Tehsil of Anantnag district in Jammu & Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Tail Wani
topPlaces.description: Tail Wani, commonly known as Tailweain, is a village in Anantnag tehsils in Anantnag district, Jammu and Kashmir, India. Tail Wani village is located in Anantnag Tehsil of Anantnag district in Jammu & Kashmir.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Martand Sun Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Martand Sun Temple is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Martand Sun Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (49 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (88 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Martand Sun Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Martand Sun Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Martand Sun Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Martand Sun Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Martand Sun Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Krewa Rampur, Chittisinghpura massacre, Bumzuva Cave and Temple, Lidder Valley, Mattan, Jammu and Kashmir.
